metric_value: Will constitute majority of development by turn of decade (~2010)
notes: Published version confirms dateline February 23 2006. Repeated from SOA1 version (d21c1b-obs-001). SOA and Web Services based on industry standards — in use at many enterprises. Cross-reference: enterprise-integration-perspective-d21c1b-obs-001.

metric_value: ~10 years for significant dividends in lower IT lifecycle costs and faster more flexible application changes
notes: Repeated from SOA1 version (d21c1b-obs-002). Cross-reference: enterprise-integration-perspective-d21c1b-obs-002. Legacy applications wrapped as SOA-based services.

metric_name: IT budget freed by best-in-class vs average
metric_value: Best-in-class IT organizations free up 15% of IT budget with lower software maintenance costs vs average
notes: Repeated from SOA1 version. Cross-reference: enterprise-integration-perspective-d21c1b-obs-003. Savings redeployed to IT innovation driving business value.

metric_value: Microsoft discarded tens of millions of lines of code in 2004 to institute programming process change enabling lower lifecycle cost
notes: Repeated from SOA1 version. Cross-reference: enterprise-integration-perspective-d21c1b-obs-004. Cited as evidence of programmer productivity crisis.

metric_value: Programmer productivity stuck at 1970s level at many shops — legacy application spaghetti-code soaks up enormous talent for trivial gains
notes: Megatrend #1. World does not have programmers coding in 3GLs needed to solve business problems at hand. SOA/composite apps/BPM identified as solutions.

metric_value: Aberdeen's first framework in 1988 — spoke-node-ring (SNR) — foreshadowed client-server and LAN/WAN revolution
notes: NEW vs. SOA1 version detail. Kastner explicitly references Aberdeen's 1988 SNR framework as intellectual predecessor to current Enterprise Integration model. Published version includes this historical context.

metric_value: The edge is gaining lots of intelligence — exemplified by growth in retail store and shop floor computing power
notes: Megatrend #3 (edge). Edge = data first captured and end-point for delivery. Integrating edge with existing infrastructure creates value generation opportunity.

metric_name: Network vendor threat to server market
metric_value: Sun and other server suppliers at risk from Cisco angling to provide intelligent network service networks
notes: Repeated from SOA1 version. Cross-reference: enterprise-integration-perspective-d21c1b-obs-007. Published version confirms February 23 2006 date for this prediction.

metric_value: Technologies contributing to KW productivity: collaboration, messaging, smart phones, laptops, podcasting, conferencing, enterprise portals, project management and compliance, ERP, SFA, CRM
notes: Megatrend #6. NEW detail — explicit list includes podcasting, enterprise portals, conferencing. Microsoft Office, ERP, SFA, CRM named. Laptops ROI example: a few minutes of e-mail time while waiting for meetings provides positive ROI.

metric_value: Best-in-class organizations show positive ROI for laptops merely from a few minutes of email time gained while waiting for meetings to start
notes: NEW detail in published version. Concrete example of KW productivity ROI. Notable for its specificity and simplicity.

metric_value: Mostly large companies with RTBI experience consider business value generation so critical that the 'how' becomes a company secret — a competitive weapon
EII is technology that promises to augment traditional data warehouse technology; data warehouses look at older information not where business is or will be
Leading companies will translate lessons from scientific massively parallel computing into a grid for commercial computing; linked to real-time operational analysis and decision-making
Mobility technology technically mature enough in 2006 but many enterprises have only tepid reactive strategy for generating business value
IT investments must be synergistic — over time worth more than sum of individual projects; integration of IT with business processes is area ripe for improving efficiency
Chip-maker focus on instructions-per-watt will allow greater density; better cooling technology lowering HVAC demands
notes: NEW detail not specifically highlighted in SOA1 extraction. Kastner identifies chip efficiency trend in context of datacenter deconstruction.

metric_value: Operational tools to remotely monitor, virtually reconfigure, and deploy servers are needed to keep labor costs under control
Enterprise WAN and related managed services may be an outsourcing candidate
notes: NEW detail in published version. Networking as outsourcing candidate — notable given the contemporary IT outsourcing themes. Network supplies encryption, compression, queuing, messaging, QoS services that today run on conventional servers.
